# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Create a multi-job GitHub Actions workflow where one job builds artifacts and subsequent jobs download and deploy them. The deployment job should download artifacts from the build job and execute deployment scripts. This is for our multi-stage deployment pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Multi-Stage Deployment

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-artifacts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up build environment
      run: |
        # Install dependencies, configure the environment, etc.
        echo "Setting up build environment..."

    - name: Build artifacts
      run: |
        # Commands to build your application
        echo "Building artifacts..."
        mkdir -p dist
        touch dist/artifact.zip  # Simulate artifact creation

    - name: Upload artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
      with:
        name: my-build-artifacts
        path: dist/

  deploy-to-dev:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-artifacts

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Download artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v2
      with:
        name: my-build-artifacts
        path: dist/

    - name: Deploy to development environment
      run: |
        # Commands to deploy the application to the dev environment
        echo "Deploying to development environment..."
        ls dist/  # List artifacts for verification

  deploy-to-prod:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: deploy-to-dev

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Download artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v2
      with:
        name: my-build-artifacts
        path: dist/

    - name: Deploy to production environment
      run: |
        # Commands to deploy the application to the prod environment
        echo "Deploying to production environment..."
        ls dist/  # List artifacts for verification
